content_preview.title: Stanford Geophysicists Produce First Global Map of Continental Mantle Earthquakes, Cataloging 459 Deep Tremors Since 1990
content_preview.summary: A Stanford-led study published in Science presents the first systematic global catalog of earthquakes originating in the continental mantle, identifying 459 events since 1990 and revealing unexpected clustering beneath the Himalayas and the Bering Strait.
content_preview.body_excerpt:
## Overview

Most earthquakes originate in Earth's crust, the relatively thin outer shell that extends down roughly 30 to 50 kilometers beneath the continents. But a small and poorly understood fraction occurs deeper, in the mantle layer that lies between the crust and the planet's molten core. A study [published on February 5, 2026, in Science](https://www.science.org/doi/10.1126/science.adz4367) by Stanford geophysicists Shiqi (Axel) Wang and Simon Klemperer now provides the first comprehensiv...

editor_notes.content_quality: Clear, well-organized science news article explaining a novel seismological method and its findings. Good balance of technical detail and broader significance.
editor_notes.source_verification: All 4 sources manually verified. Stanford Sustainability confirmed 459 continental mantle earthquakes, Sn/Lg wave technique, Shiqi Wang and Simon Klemperer as researchers, Science journal publication Feb 5 2026, clustering in Himalayas and Bering Strait, 100x less frequent than crustal quakes. Phys.org confirmed same details. ScienceDaily confirmed clustering regions including Alpine-Himalayan belt, Lake Baikal, Bering Strait, southwestern China, western US. Science DOI (10.1126/science.adz4367) confirmed.
editor_notes.factual_accuracy: All claims precisely verified: 459 earthquakes since 1990, 46,000 initial dataset narrowed to 10,770 then 459, Sn wave/Lg wave methodology, clustering patterns, Tibetan Plateau absence pattern. Wang quote about 'complete game-changer' confirmed.
editor_notes.tone_assessment: Appropriately measured scientific reporting without overselling implications.
editor_notes.originality: Clean, accessible explanation of a specialized geophysics study.
editor_notes.overall_assessment: Excellent science news reporting with all claims verified against multiple sources. Approved.
